folio.label: 130r
folio.l: And preyeden hire on knees , for goddes sake
To come and romen hire , in compaignye
A wey to dryue , hire derk fantasie
And finally she graunted , that requeste
For wel she saugú , that it was for the beste
¢ Now stood hire Castel , fast by the See
And often witú hire frendes , walketú she
Hire to disporte , vp on the banke an heigú
Where as she many a Ship~ , and barge seigú
Seylyngö here cours , where as he~ list to goo
But thanne was that , a ¹cel of hire woo
For to hire self , ful ofte allas seitú she
Is there no ship~ , of so manye as I se
Wil bryngen hom my lord , tha~ne were myn herte
Al warisshed , of hise bittre peynes smerte
¢ A nother tyme , there wold she sitte and thynke
And cast hire eighen , dounward fro the brynke
But whan she seigú , the grisly Rokkes blake
For verrey fere , so wold hire hert quake
That on hire feet , she mygút hire nougút sustene
Than wolde she sitte a doun , vp on the grene
And pitously , in to the See byholde
And seyn rigút thus , witú sorweful sikes colde
Eterne god , that thurgú thy purueaunce
Ledest the world , by eterne gouernaunce
In Ydel as men seyn , ©e no thyngö make
But lord theise grisly , fendly rokkes blake
That semen rather , a foul confusiouö
Of werk , than any fair , creacioun
Of swicú a ¹fyt wys god , and a stable
Why han ©e wrougút this werk , vn resonable
For by this werk , Soutú , Nortú , West ne Est
There nys I fostred noman , ne bryd ne beest
It dootú no good , to my wytte , but annoyetú
Se ©e nat lord , how mankynde it destroyetú
An hundred thou©sand bodyes , of man kynde
Han Rokkes slayn , al be they nougút in mynde
Whicú mankynde is so faire , a part of thy werke
That thow it madest , like to thyn owen merke
Thanne semed it , ©e had a gret chierte
Toward mankynde , but how tha~ne may it be
That ©e swicú menes make , it to destroyen
Whicú menes do no good , but euäe annoyen
I wot wel , clerkes wiln seyn as hem lest´
